year_built: 4th c. foundations; Crusader basilica 12th c.; successive rebuilds
architecture_style: Basilica; Byzantine, Romanesque, Baroque layers
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Major pilgrimage church enclosing sites tradition associates with the crucifixion and resurrection. Several Christian communities share custodial arrangements under the Status Quo
facts: The rotunda covers the edicule over the tomb; scheduling can affect access inside
The Immovable Ladder on a façade has been a symbol of intercommunal restraint since the 18th century
history: Constantine’s era complex was replaced and enlarged over centuries; the Crusaders left a lasting footprint on the Latin-rite spaces visitors see today
significance: For many Christians worldwide, this is the symbolic heart of Jerusalem’s sacred topography
description_ru: Крупная паломническая церковь, охватывающая места и традиции, связанные с распятием и воскресением. Несколько христианских общин совместно осуществляют опеку в рамках «Статус-Кво»
history_ru: Комплекс эпохи Константина был заменен и расширен на протяжении столетий; крестоносцы оставили заметный след в пространствах латинского обряда, которые видны сегодня
significance_ru: Для многих христиан по всему миру это символическое сердце священной топографии Иерусалима
facts_ru: Ротонда покрывает эдикулу над гробницей; расписание может повлиять на доступ внутрь
Лесенка (или лестница) на фасаде является символом межобщинного воздержания с XVIII века
description_en: Major pilgrimage church enclosing sites tradition associates with the crucifixion and resurrection. Several Christian communities share custodial arrangements under the Status Quo
history_en: Constantine’s era complex was replaced and enlarged over centuries; the Crusaders left a lasting footprint on the Latin-rite spaces visitors see today
significance_en: For many Christians worldwide, this is the symbolic heart of Jerusalem’s sacred topography
facts_en: The rotunda covers the edicule over the tomb; scheduling can affect access inside
The Immovable Ladder on a façade has been a symbol of intercommunal restraint since the 18th century

address: Mount of Olives, beside the Garden of Gethsemane
architecture_style: Romanesque revival basilica; mosaics facing the Rock of Agony
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Pilgrimage basilica over the shelf of bedrock where tradition locates Christ’s agony; the interior’s violet windows keep the nave dim and devotional
facts: Often called the Basilica of the Agony alongside its formal name
Olive trees in the adjoining garden are venerated though their age is debated by botanists
history: A succession of shrines occupied the site before the present building, sponsored by several Catholic communities, framed Gethsemane for mass pilgrimage in the 20th century
significance: A key Christian station on the Mount of Olives—paired with panoramic viewpoints and Jewish cemetery terraces above the Kidron
description_ru: Паломническая базилика, расположенная на скальном выступе, где, согласно преданию, находились муки Христа; фиолетовые окна интерьера придают нефу полумрак и благоговейное настроение
history_ru: На этом месте до нынешнего здания располагалась череда святилищ, спонсируемых различными католическими общинами, и придавала атмосфере Гефсиманского сада обстановку для массового паломничества в XX веке
significance_ru: Ключевое христианское место на Горице Оливной, в сочетании с панорамными смотровыми площадками и террасами еврейского кладбища над Кидроном
facts_ru: Часто называемый Базиликой Страстей Голгофы наряду с его официальным названием
Оливковые деревья в прилегающем саду почитаются, хотя их возраст оспаривается ботаниками
description_en: Pilgrimage basilica over the shelf of bedrock where tradition locates Christ’s agony; the interior’s violet windows keep the nave dim and devotional
history_en: A succession of shrines occupied the site before the present building, sponsored by several Catholic communities, framed Gethsemane for mass pilgrimage in the 20th century
significance_en: A key Christian station on the Mount of Olives—paired with panoramic viewpoints and Jewish cemetery terraces above the Kidron
facts_en: Often called the Basilica of the Agony alongside its formal name
Olive trees in the adjoining garden are venerated though their age is debated by botanists

address: Muslim Quarter near Lions’ Gate (St Stephen’s Gate)
architecture_style: Romanesque; remarkable acoustics in the triple-vaulted nave
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Best-preserved Crusader church in the city, traditionally marking the birthplace of Mary; its stone volumes reward slow listening when pilgrims sing
facts: The nearby Pools of Bethesda archaeological compound is usually visited on the same ticket
Missionaries of Africa (White Fathers) administer the church
history: Saladin converted the building to an Islamic school; restoration recovered the crusader fabric for Christian liturgy while respecting the precinct’s layered past
significance: For historians it is a textbook Romanesque survivor; for pilgrims it is Marian terrain at the edge of the Temple platform’s archaeology
description_ru: Лучше сохранившаяся крестоносная церковь в городе, традиционно отмечающая место рождения Марии; ее каменные объемы вознаграждают внимательное слушание, когда поют паломники
history_ru: Саладин превратил здание в исламскую школу; реставрация восстановила крестоносный облик для христианской литургии, уважая многослойное прошлое этого места
significance_ru: Для историков это образец романского стиля; для паломников — марианская местность на краю археологии храмовой платформы
facts_ru: Археологический комплекс «Бассейны Беф» обычно посещается по тому же билету
Миссионеры Африки (Белые отцы) управляют церковью
description_en: Best-preserved Crusader church in the city, traditionally marking the birthplace of Mary; its stone volumes reward slow listening when pilgrims sing
history_en: Saladin converted the building to an Islamic school; restoration recovered the crusader fabric for Christian liturgy while respecting the precinct’s layered past
significance_en: For historians it is a textbook Romanesque survivor; for pilgrims it is Marian terrain at the edge of the Temple platform’s archaeology
facts_en: The nearby Pools of Bethesda archaeological compound is usually visited on the same ticket
Missionaries of Africa (White Fathers) administer the church

name_en: Church of St Peter in Gallicantu
architecture_style: 20th-c. basilica; excavated first-century quarries and cisterns
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Roman Catholic complex commemorating Peter’s denial; steep staircases link upper church, chapels, and subterranean halls associated with imprisonment
facts: The crowing rooster motif appears in mosaics and signage (before the cock crow)
Scholars debate whether the crypt is the high priest’s prison; the architecture makes the debate tangible
history: Byzantine and crusader chapels preceded the contemporary basilica; excavations exposed Herodian masonry cut into the hillside
significance: A southern counterweight to the Holy Sepulchre narrative—memory of fault, repentance, and dawn
description_ru: Комплекс римско-католической церкви, посвященный отречению Петра; крутые лестницы соединяют верхнюю церковь, часовни и подземные залы, связанные с тюремным заключением
history_ru: Византийские и крестоносные часовни предшествовали современному базилике; раскопки обнаружили иудейскую кладку, высеченную в склоне холма
significance_ru: Южный противовес нарративу Святой Гроба — память о вине, покаянии и рассвете
facts_ru: Мотив кричащего петуха встречается в мозаиках и вывесках (до крика петуха)
Учёные спорят, является ли крипта тюрьмой главного жреца; архитектура делает этот спор осязаемым
description_en: Roman Catholic complex commemorating Peter’s denial; steep staircases link upper church, chapels, and subterranean halls associated with imprisonment
history_en: Byzantine and crusader chapels preceded the contemporary basilica; excavations exposed Herodian masonry cut into the hillside
significance_en: A southern counterweight to the Holy Sepulchre narrative—memory of fault, repentance, and dawn
facts_en: The crowing rooster motif appears in mosaics and signage (before the cock crow)
Scholars debate whether the crypt is the high priest’s prison; the architecture makes the debate tangible

address: Mount Zion, southwest of the Old City walls
architecture_style: Round Byzantine revival church; mosaic drum under the dome
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Benedictine abbey crowned by a rotunda where tradition locates Mary’s ‘falling asleep’; crypt statues and the upper church balance monumentality with quiet chant
facts: The community maintains the Latin liturgy while welcoming tourists on a tight schedule
Tiers of old-city roofscape open from the esplanade before you descend to Zion Gate
history: Built on land linked to medieval dormition shrines; damaged in 20th-century unrest and repaired as a symbol of German–Holy See reconciliation efforts
significance: A major Latin pilgrimage axis on Mount Zion alongside Upper Room traditions and Jewish cemeteries on the western hill
description_ru: Бенедиктинский монастырь с ротондой, где по традиции совершается «засыпание Девы Марии»; статуи в крипте и верхняя церковь сочетают монументальность с тихим пением
history_ru: Построен на землях, связанных с средневековыми святилищами Дормиции; поврежден в результате волнений XX века и восстановлен как символ усилий Германии и Святого Престола по примирению
significance_ru: Главная латинская паломническая ось на горе Сион, рядом с традициями Верхней Комнаты и еврейскими кладбищами на западном холме
facts_ru: Сообщество сохраняет латинскую литургию, одновременно принимая туристов по расписанию
Многоуровничная панорама крыш старого города открывается с эспланады, прежде чем вы спуститесь к Воротам Сиона
description_en: Benedictine abbey crowned by a rotunda where tradition locates Mary’s ‘falling asleep’; crypt statues and the upper church balance monumentality with quiet chant
history_en: Built on land linked to medieval dormition shrines; damaged in 20th-century unrest and repaired as a symbol of German–Holy See reconciliation efforts
significance_en: A major Latin pilgrimage axis on Mount Zion alongside Upper Room traditions and Jewish cemeteries on the western hill
facts_en: The community maintains the Latin liturgy while welcoming tourists on a tight schedule
Tiers of old-city roofscape open from the esplanade before you descend to Zion Gate

architecture_style: Neo-Romanesque; tall bell tower over the souk grid
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Landmark of the Evangelical Church in Jerusalem—its tower climb reveals domes and minarets in compass order; the nave reflects late Victorian Protestant taste in the Old City
facts: Combined tickets sometimes cover tower access and adjacent archaeological displays
The Muristan courtyards around it mix cafes, Orthodox choirs, and souvenir lanes
history: Erected on crusader-era ruins granted after diplomatic negotiations; it signaled Protestant permanence beside older Catholic and Orthodox jurisdictions
significance: For Lutheran and wider Protestant visitors it is the ceremonial center; for any walker it is the vertical exclamation point in the Christian quarter’s roof sea
history_ru: Возведенный на руинах крестоносцев, полученных после дипломатических переговоров; он ознаменовал протестантскую постоянность рядом со старыми католическими и православными юрисдикциями
significance_ru: Для лютеран и приверженцев более широких протестантских конфессий это церемониальный центр; а для случайного прогуливающегося — это вертикальный акцент среди крыш христианского квартала
facts_ru: Комбинированные билеты иногда включают доступ к башне и прилегающим археологическим выставкам
Дворы Муристиана сочетают кафе, православные хоры и сувенирные лавки
description_en: Landmark of the Evangelical Church in Jerusalem—its tower climb reveals domes and minarets in compass order; the nave reflects late Victorian Protestant taste in the Old City
history_en: Erected on crusader-era ruins granted after diplomatic negotiations; it signaled Protestant permanence beside older Catholic and Orthodox jurisdictions
significance_en: For Lutheran and wider Protestant visitors it is the ceremonial center; for any walker it is the vertical exclamation point in the Christian quarter’s roof sea
facts_en: Combined tickets sometimes cover tower access and adjacent archaeological displays
The Muristan courtyards around it mix cafes, Orthodox choirs, and souvenir lanes

address: Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif (access rules vary)
architecture_style: Umayyad octagon; Ottoman tile revetment (16th c.)
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Octagonal shrine with a gilt dome, built over the exposed rock at the summit of the Herodian platform. Non-Muslim visitor rules change; modest dress is expected when access is open
facts: Interior Quranic inscription bands are among the earliest monumental examples of Arabic script
The drum and dome profile became an iconic silhouette in Islamic architecture
history: The Umayyad caliphate raised the structure as a statement of legitimacy in Jerusalem; tile and marble campaigns later reframed its appearance for Ottoman taste
significance: The building anchors the visual and devotional identity of the Haram for Muslims and shapes skylines views across the Old City
description_ru: Восьмиугольный святилище с позолоченным куполом, построенный над обнаженной скалой на вершине геродианской платформы. Изменились правила посещения для немусульман; ожидается скромная одежда, когда доступ открыт
history_ru: Умайядский халифат возвёл это сооружение как доказательство своей легитимности в Иерусалиме; позже, с использованием плитки и мрамора, его внешний вид был преображён в соответствии с османским вкусом
significance_ru: Это здание является основой визуальной и религиозной идентичности Харама для мусульман и формирует панорамные виды над Старым городом
facts_ru: Интерьеры коранические надписи являются одними из самых ранних монументальных примеров арабской письменности
Профиль барабана и купола стал знаковым силуэтом в исламской архитектуре
description_en: Octagonal shrine with a gilt dome, built over the exposed rock at the summit of the Herodian platform. Non-Muslim visitor rules change; modest dress is expected when access is open
history_en: The Umayyad caliphate raised the structure as a statement of legitimacy in Jerusalem; tile and marble campaigns later reframed its appearance for Ottoman taste
significance_en: The building anchors the visual and devotional identity of the Haram for Muslims and shapes skylines views across the Old City
facts_en: Interior Quranic inscription bands are among the earliest monumental examples of Arabic script
The drum and dome profile became an iconic silhouette in Islamic architecture

year_built: Ancient village core; modern neighborhood growth 20th c.
architecture_style: Stone village lanes; hillside terraces; churches
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Hillside village-neighborhood with quiet lanes, gardens, and church complexes; a slower counterpoint to the Old City density
facts: Often visited for cafés and short scenic walks between viewpoints
Spring and early morning light make the stone textures read well in photos
history: Long a rural village outside Jerusalem’s walls, Ein Kerem became integrated into the city as western neighborhoods expanded
significance: A reset point: olive terraces, air, and scale after the Old City’s compressed streets
description_ru: Тихий район-деревня на холме с тихими улочками, садами и церковными комплексами; более спокойная альтернатива плотности Старого города
history_ru: Долгое время являвшееся сельской деревней за стенами Иерусалима, Эйн-Керем было интегрировано в город по мере расширения западных кварталов
significance_ru: Место для передышки: оливковые террасы, воздух и простор, контрастирующие с тесно застроенными улочками Старого города
facts_ru: Часто посещается ради кафе и коротких прогулок среди живописных мест
Весной и ранним утром каменная текстура хорошо смотрится на фотографиях
description_en: Hillside village-neighborhood with quiet lanes, gardens, and church complexes; a slower counterpoint to the Old City density
history_en: Long a rural village outside Jerusalem’s walls, Ein Kerem became integrated into the city as western neighborhoods expanded
significance_en: A reset point: olive terraces, air, and scale after the Old City’s compressed streets
facts_en: Often visited for cafés and short scenic walks between viewpoints
Spring and early morning light make the stone textures read well in photos

architecture_style: Neo-Byzantine revival with a prominent dome
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Landmark synagogue on the Old City plateau, rebuilt after decades as a ruin; its dome reasserted the Jewish Quarter skyline
facts: The name “Hurva” (ruin) recalls an older 18th-century congregation’s bankruptcy
The rebuilt interior revives the Ottoman-era monumental scheme
history: Cycles of building, destruction, and memorial use trace the turbulent modern history of Jerusalem’s Jewish communities
significance: A focal point for worship and for reading the quarter’s layered archaeology and memory
description_ru: Знаменитая синагога на плато Старого города, восстановленная после десятилетий пребывания в руинах; её купол восстановил панораму Еврейского квартала
history_ru: Циклы строительства, разрушения и мемориального использования отражают бурную современную историю еврейских общин Иерусалима
significance_ru: Центр паломничества и место для изучения многослойной археологии и памяти квартала
facts_ru: Название «Хурва» (руина) напоминает о банкротстве более ранней общины XVIII века
Восстановленный интерьер возрождает монументальный османский стиль
description_en: Landmark synagogue on the Old City plateau, rebuilt after decades as a ruin; its dome reasserted the Jewish Quarter skyline
history_en: Cycles of building, destruction, and memorial use trace the turbulent modern history of Jerusalem’s Jewish communities
significance_en: A focal point for worship and for reading the quarter’s layered archaeology and memory
facts_en: The name “Hurva” (ruin) recalls an older 18th-century congregation’s bankruptcy
The rebuilt interior revives the Ottoman-era monumental scheme

architecture_style: Modernist campus; Shrine of the Book nearby
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Israel’s flagship encyclopedic museum: archaeology, fine arts, Judaica, and temporary exhibitions on a terraced campus beside the parliament district
facts: The campus integrates outdoor sculpture routes and stepped gardens
The Dead Sea Scrolls are exhibited in the adjacent Shrine of the Book
history: Founded as a national collection hub; successive wings widened its role in conservation and public education
significance: The standard half-day anchor for visitors who want context before—or after—walking the Old City
description_ru: Энциклопедический музей Израиля: археология, изобразительное искусство, иудаика и временные выставки на террасном кампусе рядом с районом парламента
history_ru: Изначально основанный как национальный центр коллекций, строительство последовательных крыльев расширило его роль в сфере сохранения и публичного просвещения
significance_ru: Стандартная полудневная программа для посетителей, которые хотят получить контекст до или после прогулки по Старому городу
facts_ru: Кампус включает маршруты открытой скульптуры и многоуровневые сады
Рукописи Мертвого моря выставлены в соседнем Храме Книги
description_en: Israel’s flagship encyclopedic museum: archaeology, fine arts, Judaica, and temporary exhibitions on a terraced campus beside the parliament district
history_en: Founded as a national collection hub; successive wings widened its role in conservation and public education
significance_en: The standard half-day anchor for visitors who want context before—or after—walking the Old City
facts_en: The campus integrates outdoor sculpture routes and stepped gardens
The Dead Sea Scrolls are exhibited in the adjacent Shrine of the Book

address: Old City walls (entry points vary by season)
architecture_style: Ottoman fortifications; crenellations and towers
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Walkable sections atop the Ottoman walls that stitch together skyline views: domes, courtyards, minarets, and the city’s layered roofscape
facts: The northern and southern sections are usually ticketed separately
Bring water: sun exposure is strong and shade is scarce on the parapets
history: Suleiman’s wall circuit formalized the perimeter of today’s Old City; the ramparts later became a curated viewpoint route for visitors
significance: One of the few ways to read Jerusalem’s geometry from above—without leaving the walls
description_ru: Прогулочные участки на вершине османских стен, которые объединяют панорамные виды: купола, внутренние дворы, минареты и многосложный городской родовой пейзаж
history_ru: Комплекс стен Сулеймана определил периметр современного Старого города; эти зублы впоследствии превратились в специально организованный обзорный маршрут для посетителей
significance_ru: Одна из немногих способов рассмотреть геометрию Иерусалима с высоты — не выходя за стены
facts_ru: Северная и южная части обычно приобретаются отдельными билетами
Возьмите с собой воду: солнечное излучение сильное, а тени на парапетах мало
description_en: Walkable sections atop the Ottoman walls that stitch together skyline views: domes, courtyards, minarets, and the city’s layered roofscape
history_en: Suleiman’s wall circuit formalized the perimeter of today’s Old City; the ramparts later became a curated viewpoint route for visitors
significance_en: One of the few ways to read Jerusalem’s geometry from above—without leaving the walls
facts_en: The northern and southern sections are usually ticketed separately
Bring water: sun exposure is strong and shade is scarce on the parapets

architecture_style: Ottoman fortification with later traffic alterations
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Principal gate from western Jerusalem into the Old City; adjoins the David Street market spine toward the Church of the Holy Sepulchre
facts: Opposite lies the Tower of David museum complex inside the citadel
Street level drops toward Otto Plaza and Mamilla, changing sightlines at dusk
history: Suleiman’s wall circuit defined today’s Old City gates; the breach for a carriage road belongs to the late Ottoman modernization era
significance: Most visitors’ first tactile encounter with the Ottoman enceinte and its bustle
description_ru: Главные ворота из западного Иерусалима в Старый город; примыкает к центральной торговой оси улицы Давида в сторону Храма Гроба Господня
history_ru: Контур стен Сулеймана определил ворота современного Старого города; прорезь для каретного проезда относится к эпохе поздней османской модернизации
significance_ru: Первое знакомство большинства посетителей с османским ограждением и его суетой
facts_ru: Напротив находится музейный комплекс «Сионская цитадель» (Tower of David) внутри цитадели
Уровень улицы опускается в сторону площади Отто и Мамилли, меняя панораму в сумерках
description_en: Principal gate from western Jerusalem into the Old City; adjoins the David Street market spine toward the Church of the Holy Sepulchre
history_en: Suleiman’s wall circuit defined today’s Old City gates; the breach for a carriage road belongs to the late Ottoman modernization era
significance_en: Most visitors’ first tactile encounter with the Ottoman enceinte and its bustle
facts_en: Opposite lies the Tower of David museum complex inside the citadel
Street level drops toward Otto Plaza and Mamilla, changing sightlines at dusk

architecture_style: Neo-Byzantine / Romanesque revival tower complex
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Landmark tower and arches beside the King David Hotel; concerts, sport facilities, and language programs occupy the campus
facts: The architect also shaped New Delhi’s central avenues—compare the muscular masonry vocabulary
The vertical emphasis offers observation points over the western valley
history: Built under the British Mandate as an interfaith civic gesture with monumental stone detailing
significance: A readable Mandate-era punctuation mark between old walls and modern western districts
description_ru: Панорамная башня и аркады рядом с отелем Короля Давида; на кампусе располагаются концертные залы, спортивные объекты и языковые программы
history_ru: Построенный в период Британского мандата как межконфессиональный гражданский жест с монументальной каменной отделкой
significance_ru: Заметный «пунктуационный знак» мандатовой эпохи между старыми стенами и современными западными районами
facts_ru: Архитектор также разработал центральные проспекты Нью-Дели — сравните мускулический образец кладки
Этот выступ обеспечивает смотровые площадки над западной долиной
description_en: Landmark tower and arches beside the King David Hotel; concerts, sport facilities, and language programs occupy the campus
history_en: Built under the British Mandate as an interfaith civic gesture with monumental stone detailing
significance_en: A readable Mandate-era punctuation mark between old walls and modern western districts
facts_en: The architect also shaped New Delhi’s central avenues—compare the muscular masonry vocabulary
The vertical emphasis offers observation points over the western valley

architecture_style: Modernist public architecture; restrained monumentality
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Unicameral parliament of Israel, sited near the Israel Museum and national archive cluster in Givat Ram
facts: Tours require advance booking and ID checks
Artworks donated to the plenary include tapestries after Chagall
history: Jerusalem became the legislative seat after 1966; nearby institutions formed a deliberate civic-cultural precinct
significance: The working chamber where Israeli law is debated—paired in the same visit with the museum campus
description_ru: Однопалатный парламент Израиля, расположенный недалеко от Израильского музея и кластера национального архива в Гиват Рам
history_ru: Иерусалим стал законодательным центром после 1966 года; близлежащие учреждения сформировали продуманный гражданско-культурный квартал
significance_ru: Заседательный зал, где обсуждается израильское законодательство — в рамках одного посещения с музейным кампусом
facts_ru: Для посещения туров требуется предварительное бронирование и предъявление документов, удостоверяющих личность
Произведения искусства, пожертвованные на пленар, включают гобелены по мотивам Шагаля

history_en: Jerusalem became the legislative seat after 1966; nearby institutions formed a deliberate civic-cultural precinct
significance_en: The working chamber where Israeli law is debated—paired in the same visit with the museum campus

Artworks donated to the plenary include tapestries after Chagall

address: Between Jaffa and Agrippas streets, Nachlaot
year_built: Late Ottoman stalls; roofed phases 20th–21st c.
architecture_style: Market lanes; metal canopies; street-art façades
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Jerusalem’s busiest produce and prepared-food market by day; bars and live music crowd the lanes on Thursday nights
facts: Ethiopian spice mixes share stalls with Yemenite pastries and Balkan börek
Graffiti tours highlight portraits of local musicians and rabbis
history: Named for a late-19th-century courtyard neighborhood; the market grew along the new city’s tram and bus axes
significance: The most direct taste of contemporary Jerusalem outside the walls—loud, aromatic, and crowded
description_ru: Самый оживленный рынок свежих продуктов и готовой еды Иерусалима днем; в четверг вечером у переулков заполняют бары и живая музыка
history_ru: Названный по дворовому кварталу конца XIX века, рынок вырос вдоль новых трамвайных и автобусных осей города
significance_ru: Самый непосредственный вкус современного Иерусалима за стенами — шумный, ароматный и людный
facts_ru: Эфиопские пряные смеси соседствуют со стендами с йеменской выпечкой и балканским бореком
Туры по граффити выделяют портреты местных музыкантов и раввинов

history_en: Named for a late-19th-century courtyard neighborhood; the market grew along the new city’s tram and bus axes
significance_en: The most direct taste of contemporary Jerusalem outside the walls—loud, aromatic, and crowded
facts_en: Ethiopian spice mixes share stalls with Yemenite pastries and Balkan börek
Graffiti tours highlight portraits of local musicians and rabbis

architecture_style: European-style masonry windmill
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Restored mill overlooking the Hinnom Valley; symbol of the first Jewish neighborhood outside the Old City walls
facts: Grinding stopped when imported flour undercut local production
The ridge walk links to photography balconies toward the Old City walls
history: Financed with Montefiore’s philanthropy as part of Mishkenot Sha’ananim’s settlement experiment
significance: A readable 19th-century hinge between walled Jerusalem and expanding western neighborhoods
description_ru: Восстановленная мельница, с видом на долину Хинном; символ первого еврейского квартала за стенами Старого города
history_ru: Финансировалось благодаря благотворительности Монтефиоре в рамках эксперимента по поселению «Мишкенот-Шаананим»
significance_ru: Легко различимая (или: Явно заметная) «петля» (или: связующее звено) XIX века между стенами Иерусалима и расширяющимися западными районами
facts_ru: Производство муки прекратилось, когда импортная мука подорвала местное производство
Прогулка по хребту ведет к фотобалконам в сторону стен Старого города

architecture_style: Covered lanes, stone arches, street chapels
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Processional path associated with the Stations of the Cross, threading through markets and courtyards toward the Holy Sepulchre
facts: Stations are marked by plaques and small chapels; crowds peak on Fridays
Sections double as active shopping streets—move slowly and yield space
history: Pilgrimage itineraries shifted over centuries; the modern station sequence reflects late medieval and later devotional mapping
significance: A living overlap of worship and everyday commerce—Jerusalem’s sacred narratives embedded in ordinary streets
description_ru: Процессиональный путь, связанный со Станцией Крестного Пути, который проходит через рынки и дворы в сторону Гроба Господня
history_ru: Паломнические маршруты менялись на протяжении столетий; современная последовательность станций отражает традиции позднесредневековой и последующей паломнической картографии
significance_ru: Живой переплёт поклонения и повседневной торговли — священные нарративы Иерусалима, вплетённые в обычные улицы
facts_ru: Станции отмечены мемориальными досками и небольшими часовнями; пик скопления людей приходится на пятницу
Улицы, которые также являются активными торговыми зонами, — двигайтесь не спеша и уступайте место

year_built: From antiquity (burials, chapels); modern roads 20th c.
architecture_style: Natural ridge; scattered chapels and cemeteries
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Classic eastward view across the Kidron Valley: Jewish cemetery tiers, mosque domes, and the golden Dome of the Rock punctuating the skyline
facts: Sunrise and late afternoon change shadow depth on the Temple Mount platform
Christian sites on the slope include Dominus Flevati and the Garden of Gethsemane
history: The ridge has served burial, pilgrimage, and defensive sighting for millennia; modern roads reshape pedestrian approaches
significance: The canonical postcard perspective that shows how tightly Old Jerusalem’s sacred focal points stack together
description_ru: Классический восточный вид на долину Кидрон: уровни еврейского кладбища, купола мечети и золотой Купол Скалы, прерывающиеся на горизонте
history_ru: Гребень служил целям погребений, паломничества и оборонительного наблюдения на протяжении тысячелетий; современные дороги изменяют пешеходные подходы
significance_ru: Каноническая вид-кадр на открытке, демонстрирующий, насколько тесно расположены священные центры Старого Иерусалима
facts_ru: Восход и поздний день меняют глубину теней на платформе Храмовой Горы
На склоне расположены христианские места Доминус Флевати и Гефсиманский сад

architecture_style: Modernist; whitewashed dome over a vault
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Museum pavilion housing the Dead Sea Scrolls and related manuscript culture; the lid recalls the jars found at Qumran
facts: The white dome contrasts with a dark basalt wall evoking desert geology
Rotating exhibits complement the scroll fragments on display
history: Built as the scrolls’ permanent home after Jordanian and Israeli custody transitions; conservation labs adjoin the galleries
significance: Essential for understanding Second Temple-era Judaism before walking Herodian remains in the Old City
description_ru: Музейный павильон, где хранятся свитки Мертвого моря и связанные с ними рукописные культуры; крышка напоминает кувшины, найденные в Кумране
history_ru: Построено как постоянное место для манускриптов после передачи опеки иорданскими и израильскими властями; лаборатории консервации примыкают к галереям
significance_ru: Необходимо для понимания иудаизма периода Второго Храма перед прогулкой по геродовым остаткам в Старом городе
facts_ru: Белый купол контрастирует с тёмной базальтовой стеной, напоминающей пустынную геологию
Ротационные выставки дополняют фрагменты свитка на экспозиции

architecture_style: Contemporary memorial architecture; prism-like museum
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Israel’s official Holocaust remembrance center: museum galleries, memorials, archives, and the Hall of Names on a pine-covered ridge
facts: Plan time: the museum narrative is long and emotionally heavy—take breaks
Photography rules vary by hall; check signage and be respectful
history: Established by law as a national institution; the 2005 museum reframed the core exhibition as a chronological path through the ridge
significance: A necessary stop for historical grounding and remembrance in modern Jerusalem
description_ru: Официальный израильский центр памяти о Холокосте: музейные галереи, мемориалы, архивы и Зал имен, расположенные на сосновом хребте
history_ru: Учрежден законом в качестве национального учреждения; музей 2005 года переформатировал основную экспозицию в хронологический путь через хребет
significance_ru: Обязательная остановка для погружения в историю и памяти в современном Иерусалиме
facts_ru: Планируйте время: повествование музея длинное и эмоционально насыщенное — делайте перерывы

year_built: Herodian, Crusader, Mamluk, Ottoman layers; museum 20th c.
architecture_style: Fortress complex; minaret from Mamluk/Ottoman phases
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Citadel beside Jaffa Gate with archaeological finds and evening light shows projected on the ramparts
facts: The name “Tower of David” is traditional rather than strictly historical for the minaret
Ramparts walks reveal how Ottoman engineers tied the fort to the wall line
history: Successive empires refortified the spur controlling the western approach; the modern museum interprets those strata
significance: The best single introduction to Jerusalem’s stratigraphy without descending into tunnels—yet
description_ru: Цитадель возле Ворот Яффы с археологическими находками и вечерним световым шоу, проецируемым на крепостные стены
history_ru: Последовательные империи укрепляли мыс, контролирующий западный подход; современный музей интерпретирует эти слои
significance_ru: Наилучшее одно введение в стратиграфию Иерусалима без необходимости спуска в туннели — пока
facts_ru: Название «Башня Давида» является традиционным, а не строго историческим для минарета
Прогулка по крепостным стенам показывает, как османские инженеры связали крепость с системой городских стен

year_built: Herodian retaining wall (1st c. BCE); plaza modern
architecture_style: Herodian ashlar masonry
license_note: See Wikimedia Commons file page for license.
description: Open-air prayer place along the Herodian temenos; men’s and women’s sections follow Orthodox practice; modest dress is expected
facts: The visible courses are only part of the retaining system; tunnels continue north along the platform
Notes left in the cracks are collected periodically for respectful burial
history: The wall survived as a ruin focal point through Islamic rule; today’s plaza reflects 20th-century urban decisions after 1967
significance: For many Jews, the chief tangible link to the destroyed Temple esplanade above
description_ru: Открытая молитвенная площадка вдоль Херодиевского теменоса; мужская и женская секции следуют православной практике; ожидается скромная одежда
history_ru: Стена сохранилась в виде руин и стала центральной точкой (фокусом) в период исламского правления; современная площадь отражает решения городского планирования XX века, принятые после 1967 года
significance_ru: Для многих евреев главной материальной связью с разрушенным эспланадом Храма является
facts_ru: Видимые участки — лишь часть системы подпорных стен; туннели продолжаются на север вдоль платформы
Заметки, оставленные в трещинах, периодически собираются для достойного захоронения

license_note: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Wikimedia Commons).
description_en: The Church of the Pater Noster, located on the Mount of Olives in Jerusalem, is a significant site for Christian pilgrims. It is believed to be the place where Jesus taught his disciples the Lord's Prayer. The church features beautiful mosaics and offers stunning views of the Old City.
description: The Church of the Pater Noster, located on the Mount of Olives in Jerusalem, is a significant site for Christian pilgrims. It is believed to be the place where Jesus taught his disciples the Lord's Prayer. The church features beautiful mosaics and offers stunning views of the Old City.
history_en: The Church of the Pater Noster was originally built in the 4th century and has undergone several reconstructions, with the current structure completed in the 20th century. It is situated on a site that has long been associated with the teachings of Jesus, particularly the Lord's Prayer.
history: The Church of the Pater Noster was originally built in the 4th century and has undergone several reconstructions, with the current structure completed in the 20th century. It is situated on a site that has long been associated with the teachings of Jesus, particularly the Lord's Prayer.
significance_en: Culturally, the Church of the Pater Noster is an important pilgrimage site, attracting visitors from around the world who seek to connect with the spiritual heritage of Christianity. The church's mosaics, depicting the Lord's Prayer in various languages, reflect the universal nature of this prayer and its significance in Christian tradition.
significance: Culturally, the Church of the Pater Noster is an important pilgrimage site, attracting visitors from around the world who seek to connect with the spiritual heritage of Christianity. The church's mosaics, depicting the Lord's Prayer in various languages, reflect the universal nature of this prayer and its significance in Christian tradition.
facts_en: The church is built on the traditional site where Jesus is believed to have taught the Lord's Prayer.
It features mosaics of the Lord's Prayer in over 140 languages.
facts: The church is built on the traditional site where Jesus is believed to have taught the Lord's Prayer.
It features mosaics of the Lord's Prayer in over 140 languages.

license_note: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Wikimedia Commons).
description_en: Dominus Flevit Church, located on the Mount of Olives in Jerusalem, is a striking Roman Catholic church that commemorates the site where Jesus is believed to have wept over the city. Its unique design, resembling a teardrop, offers visitors not only a place of worship but also stunning views of the Old City. The church is a serene spot for reflection and prayer, attracting both pilgrims and tourists alike.
description: Dominus Flevit Church, located on the Mount of Olives in Jerusalem, is a striking Roman Catholic church that commemorates the site where Jesus is believed to have wept over the city. Its unique design, resembling a teardrop, offers visitors not only a place of worship but also stunning views of the Old City. The church is a serene spot for reflection and prayer, attracting both pilgrims and tourists alike.
history_en: Dominus Flevit Church was built in 1955, designed by the Italian architect Antonio Barluzzi. It stands on the site traditionally believed to be where Jesus lamented the fate of Jerusalem, as referenced in the Gospel of Luke. The church was constructed to replace an earlier chapel that had existed in the area.
history: Dominus Flevit Church was built in 1955, designed by the Italian architect Antonio Barluzzi. It stands on the site traditionally believed to be where Jesus lamented the fate of Jerusalem, as referenced in the Gospel of Luke. The church was constructed to replace an earlier chapel that had existed in the area.
significance_en: The church holds significant religious importance as it is a pilgrimage site for Christians who come to reflect on the biblical narrative of Jesus' sorrow for Jerusalem. Culturally, it is an integral part of the landscape of Jerusalem, contributing to the rich tapestry of the city's religious heritage and attracting visitors from around the world.
significance: The church holds significant religious importance as it is a pilgrimage site for Christians who come to reflect on the biblical narrative of Jesus' sorrow for Jerusalem. Culturally, it is an integral part of the landscape of Jerusalem, contributing to the rich tapestry of the city's religious heritage and attracting visitors from around the world.
facts_en: The church was completed in 1955.
It is located on the Mount of Olives, offering panoramic views of Jerusalem.
The architecture of the church is designed to resemble a teardrop.

license_note: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Wikimedia Commons).
description_en: The Pool of Bethesda, located in the Old City of Jerusalem, is an ancient site known for its historical and religious significance. This large reservoir, believed to have been constructed in the 1st century BC, is associated with healing and miracles, particularly in Christian tradition. Visitors can explore the remains of the pool and its surroundings, which offer a glimpse into the city's rich past.
description: The Pool of Bethesda, located in the Old City of Jerusalem, is an ancient site known for its historical and religious significance. This large reservoir, believed to have been constructed in the 1st century BC, is associated with healing and miracles, particularly in Christian tradition. Visitors can explore the remains of the pool and its surroundings, which offer a glimpse into the city's rich past.
history_en: The Pool of Bethesda dates back to at least the 1st century BC and was used as a water reservoir for the nearby Temple. It is mentioned in the Gospel of John as the site where Jesus healed a paralyzed man, which has contributed to its historical prominence. Archaeological excavations have revealed remnants of the pool and its associated structures, providing insights into the city's ancient water systems.
history: The Pool of Bethesda dates back to at least the 1st century BC and was used as a water reservoir for the nearby Temple. It is mentioned in the Gospel of John as the site where Jesus healed a paralyzed man, which has contributed to its historical prominence. Archaeological excavations have revealed remnants of the pool and its associated structures, providing insights into the city's ancient water systems.
significance_en: Culturally, the Pool of Bethesda is a symbol of healing and hope, deeply embedded in the Christian faith. It attracts pilgrims and tourists alike, who come to reflect on its biblical connections and the stories of miraculous healings. The site contributes to Jerusalem's status as a major religious center, fostering traditions of pilgrimage and reverence among various faith communities.
significance: Culturally, the Pool of Bethesda is a symbol of healing and hope, deeply embedded in the Christian faith. It attracts pilgrims and tourists alike, who come to reflect on its biblical connections and the stories of miraculous healings. The site contributes to Jerusalem's status as a major religious center, fostering traditions of pilgrimage and reverence among various faith communities.
facts_en: The Pool of Bethesda is located in the Old City of Jerusalem.
It is mentioned in the Gospel of John as a site of healing.
facts: The Pool of Bethesda is located in the Old City of Jerusalem.
It is mentioned in the Gospel of John as a site of healing.
stories_en: According to Christian tradition, the Pool of Bethesda was believed to have healing waters that could cure ailments, drawing many sick individuals to its shores.

license_note: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Wikimedia Commons).
description_en: The Church of the Flagellation is a significant religious site located in the heart of Jerusalem, commemorating the traditional location where Jesus was condemned to be crucified. This church is part of the Via Dolorosa, the path believed to have been taken by Jesus on his way to Calvary. Visitors can admire its beautiful architecture and the intricate artwork that depicts the passion of Christ.
description: The Church of the Flagellation is a significant religious site located in the heart of Jerusalem, commemorating the traditional location where Jesus was condemned to be crucified. This church is part of the Via Dolorosa, the path believed to have been taken by Jesus on his way to Calvary. Visitors can admire its beautiful architecture and the intricate artwork that depicts the passion of Christ.
history_en: The Church of the Flagellation was built in the 19th century, specifically in 1863, by the Franciscans. It stands on the site believed to be where Jesus was scourged and condemned before his crucifixion. The church was constructed as part of the Franciscans' effort to establish and maintain Christian holy sites in Jerusalem.
history: The Church of the Flagellation was built in the 19th century, specifically in 1863, by the Franciscans. It stands on the site believed to be where Jesus was scourged and condemned before his crucifixion. The church was constructed as part of the Franciscans' effort to establish and maintain Christian holy sites in Jerusalem.
significance_en: The Church of the Flagellation holds deep religious significance for Christians, as it marks a pivotal moment in the Passion narrative. It attracts pilgrims and tourists alike, contributing to the cultural heritage of Jerusalem. The church is also part of the broader narrative of the Via Dolorosa, which is a key pilgrimage route for many Christians.
significance: The Church of the Flagellation holds deep religious significance for Christians, as it marks a pivotal moment in the Passion narrative. It attracts pilgrims and tourists alike, contributing to the cultural heritage of Jerusalem. The church is also part of the broader narrative of the Via Dolorosa, which is a key pilgrimage route for many Christians.
facts_en: The church was built in 1863 by the Franciscans.
It is located along the Via Dolorosa, the path Jesus is believed to have taken to his crucifixion.

license_note: CC BY-SA 4.0 (Wikimedia Commons).
description_en: The Garden Tomb is a serene site located just outside the walls of the Old City of Jerusalem, believed by many to be the burial and resurrection site of Jesus Christ. Surrounded by lush gardens, it offers a peaceful atmosphere for reflection and prayer. Visitors can explore the tomb itself and enjoy the beauty of the surrounding landscape.
description: The Garden Tomb is a serene site located just outside the walls of the Old City of Jerusalem, believed by many to be the burial and resurrection site of Jesus Christ. Surrounded by lush gardens, it offers a peaceful atmosphere for reflection and prayer. Visitors can explore the tomb itself and enjoy the beauty of the surrounding landscape.
history_en: The Garden Tomb was discovered in 1867 by General Charles Gordon, who proposed it as the site of Jesus' burial. The area has been a place of Christian pilgrimage since its identification, although it is not universally accepted as the authentic burial site, with the Church of the Holy Sepulchre being the more traditional location.
history: The Garden Tomb was discovered in 1867 by General Charles Gordon, who proposed it as the site of Jesus' burial. The area has been a place of Christian pilgrimage since its identification, although it is not universally accepted as the authentic burial site, with the Church of the Holy Sepulchre being the more traditional location.
significance_en: The Garden Tomb holds profound religious significance for many Christians who view it as a symbol of Jesus' resurrection. It serves as a site of pilgrimage and worship, attracting visitors from around the world who seek to connect with their faith in a tranquil setting. The site also contributes to Jerusalem's rich cultural heritage, reflecting the city's diverse religious history.

description_en: Damascus Gate, one of the most iconic entrances to the Old City of Jerusalem, is renowned for its stunning architecture and rich history. This monumental gateway, with its striking stone facade and intricate details, serves as a bustling hub of activity, connecting various neighborhoods and cultures. Visitors can explore the vibrant market nearby and enjoy the lively atmosphere that surrounds this historic landmark.

history_en: The Damascus Gate dates back to the 16th century and was built during the Ottoman period. It is situated along the ancient road leading to Damascus, which has historically made it a significant point of entry into Jerusalem. The gate has witnessed numerous key events throughout history, including military campaigns and pilgrimages.

description_en: Mount Zion, a prominent hill located just outside the walls of the Old City of Jerusalem, is a site of profound historical and religious significance. It is home to several key landmarks, including the Tomb of King David and the Room of the Last Supper. Visitors can explore its rich heritage, which reflects the diverse cultural and spiritual tapestry of Jerusalem.
description: Mount Zion, a prominent hill located just outside the walls of the Old City of Jerusalem, is a site of profound historical and religious significance. It is home to several key landmarks, including the Tomb of King David and the Room of the Last Supper. Visitors can explore its rich heritage, which reflects the diverse cultural and spiritual tapestry of Jerusalem.
history_en: Mount Zion has been a focal point of Jerusalem's history since ancient times. It is traditionally associated with the biblical King David, who established it as his city around 1000 BCE. Over the centuries, it has witnessed numerous events, including the Crusades and various conquests, shaping its identity as a symbol of resilience and faith.
history: Mount Zion has been a focal point of Jerusalem's history since ancient times. It is traditionally associated with the biblical King David, who established it as his city around 1000 BCE. Over the centuries, it has witnessed numerous events, including the Crusades and various conquests, shaping its identity as a symbol of resilience and faith.
significance_en: Culturally, Mount Zion is a melting pot of traditions and practices, reflecting the diverse communities that have inhabited Jerusalem. It plays a significant role in Jewish, Christian, and Islamic heritage, making it a vital site for pilgrimage and religious observance. The area's historical landmarks contribute to its recognition as a UNESCO World Heritage site, underscoring its importance in the collective memory of humanity.

description_en: The Church of the Visitation, located in the picturesque hills of Ein Karem, is a significant site commemorating the visit of the Virgin Mary to her cousin Elizabeth. This beautiful church is renowned for its stunning architecture and vibrant mosaics, making it a must-visit for those exploring the spiritual and historical landscape of Jerusalem.
description: The Church of the Visitation, located in the picturesque hills of Ein Karem, is a significant site commemorating the visit of the Virgin Mary to her cousin Elizabeth. This beautiful church is renowned for its stunning architecture and vibrant mosaics, making it a must-visit for those exploring the spiritual and historical landscape of Jerusalem.
history_en: The Church of the Visitation was built in 1955 on the site traditionally believed to be where Mary visited Elizabeth, as described in the Gospel of Luke. The church is constructed over earlier Byzantine and Crusader churches, reflecting the long-standing religious significance of the location.
